{"step_by_step_thinking": "Porcelain is a ceramic material made by firing a mixture of clay and flux (usually silica and feldspar) at a high temperature. Silica and feldspar are key components in the formation of porcelain. Calcium carbonate is not typically a component of porcelain as it does not melt at the high temperatures required for porcelain production. Amorphous material can be present in porcelain as it can form during the cooling process after high-temperature firing.", "answer_choice": "C"}
